Now that annotations are in place, re-deploy the schema and restart the server:

cds deploy --to sqlite
cds watch

Open your browser at: http://localhost:4004

You will see the familiar CDS welcome page. This time, click "Fiori preview" next to the "Incidents" entity under the /odata/v4/processor service.

 

The List Report and Object page now renders with full layout and labels.

 

 


SUMMARY

In Part 2, we successfully:

✔ Created an "annotations" folder under app/ with three CDS files
✔ Added field labels for all entities (labels.cds)
✔ Defined the List Report columns, filter bar, Object Page layout and detail page sections (layout.cds)
✔ Configured value help dropdowns for Status, Urgency, and Customer (valuehelps.cds)
✔ Previewed the fully rendered Fiori Elements UI locally with SQLite

In Part 3 , we will set up SAP HANA Cloud on BTP — creating the database instance and mapping it to our Cloud Foundry space.

entry.title: Fullstack CAP Application with HANA Cloud (Decoupled Architecture) [Part-3]
entry.content:
Setting Up SAP HANA Cloud on BTP

In Part 2 , we added CDS Annotations and previewed the Fiori UI locally with SQLite. Now it is time to move to the cloud database layer.

In this Part, we will set up SAP HANA Cloud on SAP BTP so our CAP application can use it as the production database. This involves several one-time BTP configuration steps before we can deploy anything.

Here is what we will do in this part:

1. Verify SAP HANA Cloud is entitled at the Global Account level
2. Assign the HANA Cloud service plans to your Subaccount
3. Subscribe to HANA Cloud Tools via the Service Marketplace
4. Assign the required HANA Cloud roles to your user
5. Create a SAP HANA Cloud database instance
6. Map the Cloud Foundry environment to the HANA Cloud instance

All of these steps are done in the SAP BTP Cockpit and SAP HANA Cloud Central.

STEP 1 — VERIFY HANA CLOUD ENTITLEMENTS IN THE GLOBAL ACCOUNT

Before creating any HANA Cloud resources, confirm that your Global Account has the SAP HANA Cloud service entitlement available.

In the SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to:

Global Account → Entitlements → Service Assignments

In the search box, type "HANA" to filter the services.

 You should see the services available.

 

If you see these listed, your Global Account is entitled.

Proceed to Step 2.

NOTE: If SAP HANA Cloud does not appear here, contact your BTP Global Account administrator to enable the entitlement.


STEP 2 — ASSIGN HANA CLOUD SERVICE PLANS TO YOUR SUBACCOUNT

Entitlements available at the Global Account level must be explicitly assigned to a Subaccount before they can be used there.

Navigate to:

Global Account → Entitlements → Entity Assignments

Filter by your subaccount (e.g., "trial") and locate the SAP HANA Cloud service. Click "Edit" and add the required service plans to your subaccount.

- Search for "hana" in the services list on the left
- Select "SAP HANA Cloud" from the results
- On the right panel, check the following plans and Click "Add Service Plans" to confirm

 

After saving, the Entity Assignments page will show SAP HANA Cloud with the assigned plans for your subaccount.

 

STEP 3 — SUBSCRIBE TO HANA CLOUD TOOLS IN THE SERVICE MARKETPLACE

SAP HANA Cloud is managed through a browser-based graphical tool called SAP HANA Cloud Central. To access it, you must first subscribe to the "hana-cloud-tools" application in your subaccount's Service Marketplace.

Navigate to your Subaccount, then go to: Services → Service Marketplace

Search for "HANA CLOUD". Click on the "SAP HANA Cloud" tile.

 

 

In the right panel, click "Create". A wizard dialog will open.

 

 Click "Create". This creates a subscription to HANA Cloud Central.

 

Navigate to:  Services → Instances and Subscriptions

You should now see "SAP HANA Cloud" listed under Subscriptions with Status: "Subscribed".

 

STEP 4 — ASSIGN HANA CLOUD ROLES TO YOUR USER

Before you can open HANA Cloud Central, your BTP user needs the correct role collections assigned. Without them, you will see a "Not authorized" error when trying to access HANA Cloud Central.

 

 

 

The required SAP HANA Cloud role collections are:
https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/role-collections-for-sap-hana-cloud

 

To assign these roles, navigate to your Subaccount:

 

Security → Users

Click on your user  to open the details panel.
Click "Assign Role Collection".

 

 

In the "Assign Role Collection" dialog, search for "SAP HANA CLOUD".
Select all the required role collections:

 

Click "Assign Role Collection" to save.

 

STEP 5 — CREATE THE SAP HANA CLOUD DATABASE INSTANCE

Now open SAP HANA Cloud Central. You can access it from:

Services → Instances and Subscriptions → SAP HANA Cloud → "Go to Application"

HANA Cloud Central will open in a new browser tab. The Instances page will show "No instances" — we need to create one.

 

Click "Create Instance". A multi-step wizard opens with 8 steps.

1

 

2

 

3

 

4

 

5

 

6

 

7

 

8

 

After creation, HANA Cloud Central will show both instances as "Running":

 

STEP 6 — MAP THE CLOUD FOUNDRY ENVIRONMENT TO THE HANA CLOUD INSTANCE

The HANA Cloud instance was created but it needs to be mapped to your Cloud Foundry organization and space. This mapping is what enables CAP to create HDI (HANA Deployment Infrastructure) containers for schema deployment when you run cds deploy --to hana.

Click on the "SHC_DB" instance to open its details. 

 

Click "Manage Configuration" → go to the "Instance Mapping" tab.

Click "Add Mapping". You will be redirected to sign into the CF environment.

 

Sign in with your default identity provider credentials. After authentication, return to the Instance Mapping tab and click "Add Mapping" again.

 

Now a new row appears. Fill in the mapping:

Click "Review and Save" to confirm the mapping. The HANA Cloud instance is now connected to your Cloud Foundry space and ready to receive HDI container deployments from the CAP application.

 


SUMMARY

In Part 3, we successfully:

✔ Verified SAP HANA Cloud entitlements at the Global Account level
✔ Assigned HANA Cloud service plans to the trial subaccount
✔ Subscribed to SAP HANA Cloud Tools (hana-cloud-tools) via Service Marketplace
✔ Assigned all required SAP HANA Cloud role collections to our user
✔ Created a SAP HANA Cloud database instance (SHC_DB) with a linked Data Lake (SHC_DL) using the free tier
✔ Mapped the Cloud Foundry organization and space to the HANA instance to enable HDI container creation

The SAP HANA Cloud infrastructure is now fully set up and ready. In Part 4, we will connect the CAP application to this HANA Cloud instance and deploy the backend to Cloud Foundry.
